speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: You know since Pentecost the sum is due,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: And since I have not much importuned you,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Nor now I had not, but that I am bound

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: To Persia and want guilders for my voyage.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Therefore make present satisfaction,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Or I’ll attach you by this officer.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Even just the sum that I do owe to you

speaker: ANGELO
text: Is growing to me by Antipholus.

speaker: ANGELO
text: And in the instant that I met with you,

speaker: ANGELO
text: He had of me a chain. At five o’clock

speaker: ANGELO
text: I shall receive the money for the same.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Pleaseth you walk with me down to his house,

speaker: ANGELO
text: I will discharge my bond and thank you too.

speaker: OFFICER
text: That labor may you save. See where he comes.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: While I go to the goldsmith’s house, go thou

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And buy a rope’s end. That will I bestow

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Among my wife and her confederates

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For locking me out of my doors by day.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But soft. I see the goldsmith. Get thee gone.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Buy thou a rope, and bring it home to me.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I buy a thousand pound a year! I buy a rope!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A man is well holp up that trusts to you!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I promisèd your presence and the chain,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But neither chain nor goldsmith came to me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Belike you thought our love would last too long

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If it were chained together, and therefore came not.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Saving your merry humor, here’s the note

speaker: ANGELO
text: How much your chain weighs to the utmost carat,

speaker: ANGELO
text: The fineness of the gold, and chargeful fashion,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Which doth amount to three-odd ducats more

speaker: ANGELO
text: Than I stand debted to this gentleman.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I pray you, see him presently discharged,

speaker: ANGELO
text: For he is bound to sea, and stays but for it.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I am not furnished with the present money.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Besides, I have some business in the town.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Good signior, take the stranger to my house,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And with you take the chain, and bid my wife

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Disburse the sum on the receipt thereof.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Perchance I will be there as soon as you.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Then you will bring the chain to her yourself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: No, bear it with you lest I come not time enough.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Well, sir, I will. Have you the chain about you?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: An if I have not, sir, I hope you have,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Or else you may return without your money.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Nay, come, I pray you, sir, give me the chain.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Both wind and tide stays for this gentleman,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And I, to blame, have held him here too long.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Good Lord! You use this dalliance to excuse

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Your breach of promise to the Porpentine.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I should have chid you for not bringing it,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But, like a shrew, you first begin to brawl.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: The hour steals on. I pray you, sir, dispatch.

speaker: ANGELO
text: You hear how he importunes me. The chain!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Why, give it to my wife, and fetch your money.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Come, come. You know I gave it you even now.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Either send the chain, or send by me some token.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Fie, now you run this humor out of breath.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Come, where’s the chain? I pray you, let me see it.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: My business cannot brook this dalliance.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Good sir, say whe’er you’ll answer me or no.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: If not, I’ll leave him to the Officer.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I answer you? What should I answer you?

speaker: ANGELO
text: The money that you owe me for the chain.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I owe you none till I receive the chain.

speaker: ANGELO
text: You know I gave it you half an hour since.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You gave me none. You wrong me much to say so.

speaker: ANGELO
text: You wrong me more, sir, in denying it.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Consider how it stands upon my credit.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MERCHANT
text: Well, officer, arrest him at my suit.

speaker: OFFICER
text: I do, and charge you in the Duke’s name to obey me.

speaker: ANGELO
text: This touches me in reputation.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Either consent to pay this sum for me,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Or I attach you by this officer.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Consent to pay thee that I never had?—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Arrest me, foolish fellow, if thou dar’st.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Here is thy fee. Arrest him, officer.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I would not spare my brother in this case

speaker: ANGELO
text: If he should scorn me so apparently.

speaker: OFFICER
text: I do arrest you, sir. You hear the suit.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I do obey thee till I give thee bail.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text:
To
                 
                Angelo
                .
                 
              But, sirrah, you shall buy this sport as dear

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: As all the metal in your shop will answer.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Sir, sir, I shall have law in Ephesus,

speaker: ANGELO
text: To your notorious shame, I doubt it not.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Master, there’s a bark of Epidamium

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That stays but till her owner comes aboard,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And then, sir, she bears away. Our fraughtage, sir,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I have conveyed aboard, and I have bought

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The oil, the balsamum, and aqua vitae.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The ship is in her trim; the merry wind

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Blows fair from land. They stay for naught at all

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But for their owner, master, and yourself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: How now? A madman? Why, thou peevish sheep,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: What ship of Epidamium stays for me?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: A ship you sent me to, to hire waftage.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou drunken slave, I sent thee for a rope

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And told thee to what purpose and what end.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: You sent me for a rope’s end as soon.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: You sent me to the bay, sir, for a bark.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I will debate this matter at more leisure

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And teach your ears to list me with more heed.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To Adriana, villain, hie thee straight.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Give her this key, and tell her in the desk

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That’s covered o’er with Turkish tapestry

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There is a purse of ducats. Let her send it.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Tell her I am arrested in the street,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And that shall bail me. Hie thee, slave. Begone.—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: On, officer, to prison till it come.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: To Adriana. That is where we dined,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where Dowsabel did claim me for her husband.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: She is too big, I hope, for me to compass.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thither I must, although against my will,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For servants must their masters’ minds fulfill.
